title: Math patterns
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including ones in the addition or multiplication tables), and explain them using properties of operations.
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic D: Multiplication and division using units of 9

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows the number of laps Carly ran around the track each day. 

Day | Number of Laps
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$2$ |$6$
$3$ |$9$
$4$ |$12$
$5$ |$15$

**Which rule describes the pattern shown by the number of laps Carly ran?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.


[[☃ image 1]]

The pattern starts at $6$ and adds $3$ each time.

[[☃ image 1]]

The rule for the pattern $6, 9, 12, 15$ is $\purpleC{\text{add 3}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the pattern below:

$2, 6, 10, 14, ...$

**If the pattern continues, what will be the $6^{\text{th}}$ number in the pattern?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$2\underset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}6\underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 10 \underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 14$

The pattern starts at $2$ and adds $4$ each time.

$2\underset{\blueD{+~4}}{{\rightarrow}}6\underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} 10 \underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} 14$

So, if we want to know what the $6$th number will be, we need to extend the pattern:

$\overset{\greenD1}2\underset{\blueD{+~4}}{{\rightarrow}}\overset{\greenD2}6\underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD3}{10} \underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD4} {14} \underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD5}{18} \underset{\blueD{+~4}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD6}{22}$
The $6^{\text{th}}$ number in the pattern will be $22$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the pattern below:

$20, 40, 60, 80, ...$

**If the pattern continues, what will be the $8^{\text{th}}$ number in the pattern?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$20\underset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}40\underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 60 \underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 80$

The pattern starts at $20$ and adds $20$ each time.

$20\underset{\blueD{+~20}}{{\rightarrow}}40\underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} 60 \underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} 80$

So, if we want to know what the $8$th number will be, we need to extend the pattern:

$\overset{\greenD1}{20}\underset{\blueD{+~20}}{{\rightarrow}}\overset{\greenD2}{40}\underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD3}{60} \underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD4} {80} \underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD5}{100} \underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD6}{120} \underset{\blueD{+~20}} {\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD7}{140} \underset{\blueD{+~20}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD8}{160}$
The $8^{\text{th}}$ number in the pattern will be $160$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the pattern below:

$2, 4, 8, 16, ...$

**If the pattern continues, what will be the next number in the pattern?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$2\underset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}4\underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 8 \underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 16$

The pattern starts at $2$ and multiplies by $2$ each time.

$2\underset{\blueD{\times2}}{{\rightarrow}}4\underset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} 8 \underset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} 16$

So, if we want to know what the next number will be, we need to extend the pattern:

$\overset{\greenD1}2\underset{\blueD{\times2}}{{\rightarrow}}\overset{\greenD2}4\underset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD3}{8} \underset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD4} {16}\underset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD5}{32}$
The next number in the pattern will be $32$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the pattern below:

$1, 3, 9, 27, ...$

**If the pattern continues, what will be the next number in the pattern?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$1\underset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}3\underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 9 \underset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 27$

The pattern starts at $1$ and multiplies by $3$ each time.

$1\underset{\blueD{\times3}}{{\rightarrow}}3\underset{\blueD{\times3}}{\rightarrow} 9 \underset{\blueD{\times3}}{\rightarrow} 27$

So, if we want to know what the next number will be, we need to extend the pattern:

$\overset{\greenD1}1\underset{\blueD{\times3}}{{\rightarrow}}\overset{\greenD2}3\underset{\blueD{\times3}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD3}{9} \underset{\blueD{\times3}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD4} {27} \underset{\blueD{\times3}}{\rightarrow} \overset{\greenD5}{81}$
The next number in the pattern will be $81$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression can we use to find the missing number in the pattern?**

$36, 45, \_\_\_, 63, 72, 81$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$36\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}45\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} ~? \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 63\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}72 \overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}81$

The pattern is to start at $36$ and add $9$ each time.

$36\overset{\blueD{+~9}}{{\rightarrow}}45\overset{\blueD{+~9}}{\rightarrow} ~\goldD? \overset{\blueD{+~9}}{\rightarrow} 63\overset{\blueD{+~9}}{{\rightarrow}}72 \overset{\blueD{+~9}}{{\rightarrow}}81$

To find the missing number, we need to $\blueD{\text{add 9}}$ to $45$. 

$36{{\rightarrow}}45\overset{\blueD{+~9}}{\rightarrow} ~\goldD{54} {\rightarrow} 63{{\rightarrow}}72 {{\rightarrow}}81$
The expression that helps us find the missing number is $45 + 9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression can we use to find the missing number in the pattern?**

$30, 45, 60,  \_\_\_,  90, 105$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$30\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}45\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} ~60 \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} \goldD?\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}90 \overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}105$

The pattern is to start at $30$ and add $15$ each time.

$30\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}45\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}} 60 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} \goldD?\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}90 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}105$

To find the missing number, we need to $\blueD{\text{add 15}}$ to $60$. 

$30{{\longrightarrow}}45{\longrightarrow} ~{60}\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} \goldD{75}{{\longrightarrow}}90 {{\longrightarrow}}105$
The expression that helps us find the missing number is $60 + 15$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression can we use to find the missing number in the pattern?**

$3, 6, 12,  \_\_\_,  48, 96$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

$3\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}6\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} ~12 \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} \goldD?\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}48 \overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}96$

The pattern is to start at $3$ and multiply by $2$ each time.

$3\overset{\blueD{\times2}}{{\rightarrow}}6\overset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} ~12 \overset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} \goldD?\overset{\blueD{\times2}}{{\rightarrow}}48 \overset{\blueD{\times2}}{{\rightarrow}}96$

To find the missing number, we need to $\blueD{\text{multiply 2}}$ and $12$. 

$3{{\rightarrow}}6{\rightarrow} ~{12}\overset{\blueD{\times2}}{\rightarrow} \goldD{24}{{\rightarrow}}48 {{\rightarrow}}96$
The expression that helps us find the missing number is $12\times2$.

The table below shows the number of laps Sam ran around the track each day. 

Day | Number of Laps
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$2$ |$6$

**If the pattern continues, how many laps will Sam run around the track on day $8$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the pattern that connects each number.

[[☃ image 1]]

The pattern starts at $6$ and adds $3$ each time.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, if we want to know how many laps Sam will run around the track on day $8$, we need to extend the pattern to the $8^{\text{th}}$ day. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Sam will run $24$ laps on day $8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rule describes the pattern shown?**

$3, 6, 12, 24$ 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see if we can find the pattern that connects each number.

$3\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}6\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 12 \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 24$

The pattern is to start at $3$ and multiply by $2$ each time.

$3\overset{\blueD{\times~2}}{{\rightarrow}}6\overset{\blueD{\times~2}}{\rightarrow} 12 \overset{\blueD{\times~2}}{\rightarrow} 24$
The rule for the pattern $3, 6, 12, 24$ is start with $3$ and $\blueD {\text{multiply by } 2}$ each time.

choices.content: Start with $3$ and add $3$ each time
Start with $3$ and add $6$ each time
Start with $3$ and multiply by $2$ each time
Start with $3$ and multiply by $3$ each time

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rule describes the pattern shown?**

$1, 4, 16, 64$ 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see if we can find the pattern that connects each number.

$1\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}4\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 16 \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 64$

The pattern is to start at $1$ and multiply by $4$ each time.

$1\overset{\blueD{\times4}}{{\rightarrow}}4\overset{\blueD{\times4}}{\rightarrow} 16 \overset{\blueD{\times4}}{\rightarrow} 64$
The rule for the pattern $1, 4, 16, 64$   is $\blueD{\text{multiply by } 4}$ each time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rule describes the pattern shown?**

$3, 15, 27, 39$ 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see if we can find the pattern that connects each number.

$3\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}15\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 27\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 39$

The pattern is to start at $3$ and add $12$ each time.

$3\overset{\blueD{+~ 12}}{{\longrightarrow}}15\overset{\blueD{+~ 12}}{\longrightarrow} 27\overset{\blueD{+~ 12}}{\longrightarrow} 39$
The rule for the pattern $3, 15, 27, 39$  is $\blueD {\text{add } 12}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which rule describes the pattern shown?**

$4, 24, 44, 64$ 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see if we can find the pattern that connects each number.

$4\overset{\blueD?}{{\rightarrow}}24\overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 44 \overset{\blueD?}{\rightarrow} 64$

The pattern is to start at $4$ and add $20$ each time.

$4\overset{\blueD{+~ 20}}{{\longrightarrow}}24\overset{\blueD{+~ 20}}{\longrightarrow} 44 \overset{\blueD{+~ 20}}{\longrightarrow} 64$
The rule for the pattern $4, 24, 44, 64$ is start with $4$ and $\blueD {\text{add } 20}$ each time.

choices.content: Start with $4$ and add $10$ each time
Start with $4$ and add $20$ each time
Start with $4$ and multiply by $4$ each time
Start with $4$ and multiply by $6$ each time

assessment_items.question_content:
The rule for the pattern shown is add $8$.  

**Fill in the missing numbers.**
 
$5$, [[☃ numeric-input 1]], $21$, $29$, [[☃ numeric-input 2]], $45$
hints:
Let's apply the pattern to find the missing numbers.

$5\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 21 \overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 29 \overset{\blueD{+~8}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 45$

The pattern is to start at $5$ and add $8$ each time.

$5\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{13}\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 21 \overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 29 \overset{\blueD{+~8}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{37}\overset{\blueD{+~8}}{\rightarrow} 45$
The pattern is $5, \goldD{13}, 21, 29, \goldD{37}, 45$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The rule for the pattern shown is add $15$.  

**Fill in the missing numbers.**
 
$3$, [[☃ numeric-input 1]], $33$, $48$, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's apply the pattern to find the missing numbers.

$3\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}\goldD{~?}\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} 33 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} 48 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}\goldD{~?}$

The pattern is to start at $3$ and add $15$ each time.

$3\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}\goldD{18}\overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} 33 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{\longrightarrow} 48 \overset{\blueD{+~15}}{{\longrightarrow}}\goldD{63}$
The pattern is $3, \goldD{18}, 33, 48, \goldD{63}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The rule for the pattern shown is multiply by $2$.  

**Fill in the missing numbers.**
 
$2$,  [[☃ numeric-input 1]], $8$, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's apply the pattern to find the missing numbers.

$2\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{\rightarrow} 8\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}$

The pattern is to start at $2$ and multiply by $2$ each time.

$2\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{4}\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{\rightarrow} 8\overset{\blueD{\times 2~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{16}$
The pattern is $2, \goldD{4}, 8, \goldD{16}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The rule for the pattern shown is multiply by $3$.  

**Fill in the missing numbers.**
 
$1$,  [[☃ numeric-input 1]], $9$, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's apply the pattern to find the missing numbers.

$1\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{\rightarrow} 9\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{~?}$

The pattern is to start at $1$ and multiply by $3$ each time.

$1\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{3}\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{\rightarrow} 9\overset{\blueD{\times 3~}}{{\rightarrow}}\goldD{27}$
The pattern is $1, \goldD{3}, 9, \goldD{27}$.
